page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and the AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core and memory performance. The r8gn.12xlarge achieves top-tier results in memory bandwidth, reaching 429.61 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 209.89 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Multi-core compute capabilities are also notable, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 23591.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 47475.08, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Additionally, static web serving throughput reaches 94.95 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations perform at 99.9 million operations per second.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, this instance is optimized for high-throughput network and EBS storage operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth relative to its power profile, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances. The absence of local storage means workloads must utilize network-attached storage, which is supported by the high-speed network interface. This server is optimized for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides a robust 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput. It also demonstrates strong multi-core performance in software compilation and Redis operations. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput network applications.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and one-hundred-fifty gigabits per second baseline network performance.

Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, delivering 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Provides 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput EBS and network connectivity.
